content.problemStatement:
To access elements in a 2D array, you use the row and column indices. \
Remember that indices start from 0. \
For example, to access the element in the second row and third column of the matrix array:
```cpp
int element = matrix[1][2]; // Row 1, Column 2
```

You can change elements in a 2D array by accessing the specific element using its row and column indices and assigning a new value to it. Here's the general syntax for changing elements in a 2D array:
```cpp
arrayName[rowIndex][columnIndex] = newValue;
```

### Task
Write a code which takes the given matrix as input. \
Change all elements of the input matrix such that each element in the new matrix is twice of the original matrix.\
Check the sample output below.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
    - The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$ — the number of elements in the array originally.
    - The next line contains $N$ space-separated integers — $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$, which are the original array elements.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of elements that she has to remove from the array.
